---
title: Tera
variables:
  version: 0.0.1-alpha.2
  urls:
    docs: https://github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin
---

::: {.callout-tip}
Click the `</>` button in the top navigation bar to view the original `.qmd` source code.
:::

*Calepin* processes the body of every `.qmd` document through the [Tera](https://keats.github.io/tera/) template engine. This lets you use expressions, functions, conditionals, and loops directly in your text. Fenced code blocks and inline code spans are automatically protected from Tera evaluation.

::: {.callout-note}
Tera expressions inside fenced code blocks and inline code spans are not evaluated by default. To opt-in to Tera processing inside a code chunk, use the `#| tera: true` chunk option:
:::

````
```{r}
#| tera: true
n <- {{var.sample_size}}
x <- rnorm(n)
summary(x)
```
````


# Syntax

Tera expressions use double braces: `{% raw %}{{ expression }}{% endraw %}`. Functions use parentheses with keyword arguments:

```
{% raw %}{{ function_name(arg="value") }}{% endraw %}
```

To include literal Tera syntax without evaluation, wrap it in raw blocks:

````
{% raw %}{% raw %}{{ this is not evaluated }}{% endraw %}{% endraw %}
````


# Context variables

## meta

Access YAML front matter fields:

{% raw %}
- `{{ meta.title }}` --- document title
- `{{ meta.author }}` --- author (comma-joined if multiple)
- `{{ meta.date }}` --- date
- `{{ meta.subtitle }}` --- subtitle
- `{{ meta.abstract }}` --- abstract text
- `{{ meta.keywords }}` --- keywords (comma-joined)
{% endraw %}

Any custom field in the YAML front matter is also available.

The title of this document is: {{ meta.title }}.

## var

Look up a variable from the `variables:` block in front matter (or in `_calepin.yaml` for site-wide variables). Uses dot-notation for nested values. For example, this document's front matter contains:

```yaml
variables:
  version: 0.0.1-alpha.2
  urls:
    docs: https://github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin
```

{% raw %}The syntax `{{ var.version }}` inserts the value.{% endraw %}

* *Calepin* version: {{ var.version }}
* Documentation URL: {{ var.urls.docs }}

## env

System environment variables are available under `env`:

{% raw %}`{{ env.SHELL }}`{% endraw %}

The `SHELL` environment variable on the local machine is: {{ env.SHELL }}.

## format

The current output format is available as `{% raw %}{{ format }}{% endraw %}` --- one of `html`, `latex`, `typst`, or `markdown`.


# Conditionals and loops

Because the document body is a Tera template, you can use control flow:

````
{% raw %}{% if format == "html" %}
This paragraph only appears in HTML output.
{% endif %}{% endraw %}
````

````
{% raw %}{% for item in var.features %}
- {{ item }}
{% endfor %}{% endraw %}
````

See the [Tera documentation](https://keats.github.io/tera/docs/) for the full template syntax, including filters, macros, and inheritance.


# Built-in functions

## pagebreak

Insert a native page break. Produces format-appropriate output: `<div style="page-break-after: always;">` in HTML, `\newpage{}` in LaTeX, `#pagebreak()` in Typst.

Page one content.

{% raw %}`{{ pagebreak() }}`{% endraw %}

Page two content.

## video

Embed a video or streaming service. Supports YouTube, Vimeo, and direct video URLs:

```
{% raw %}{{ video(url="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ") }}
{{ video(url="https://vimeo.com/123456", width="640", height="480", title="My Video") }}{% endraw %}
```

YouTube and Vimeo URLs are automatically converted to embed URLs.

## brand

Access branding information from the `brand:` block in front matter (or in `_calepin.yaml` for site-wide branding):

{% raw %}
- `{{ brand(type="color", name="primary") }}` --- returns a color value
- `{{ brand(type="color", name="primary", mode="dark") }}` --- dark mode variant
- `{{ brand(type="logo", name="mark") }}` --- returns format-appropriate logo markup
{% endraw %}

See [brand.yml specification](https://posit-dev.github.io/brand-yml/) for the structure of the `brand:` block.

## kbd

Render keyboard shortcuts:

{% raw %}`{{ kbd(keys=["Ctrl", "C"]) }}`{% endraw %}

Produces `<kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>C</kbd>` in HTML, `\texttt{Ctrl}+\texttt{C}` in LaTeX.

## lipsum

Insert placeholder lorem ipsum text. Default: 1 paragraph.

```
{% raw %}{{ lipsum() }}
{{ lipsum(paragraphs=3) }}
{{ lipsum(sentences=5) }}
{{ lipsum(words=20) }}{% endraw %}
```

Example with 2 sentences: {{ lipsum(sentences=2) }}

## placeholder

Insert a placeholder image for layout prototyping. Produces an inline SVG in HTML, `\fbox` in LaTeX, `#rect` in Typst.

```
{% raw %}{{ placeholder() }}
{{ placeholder(width=300, height=200) }}
{{ placeholder(width=400, height=100, text="Banner", color="#e0e0ff") }}{% endraw %}
```

Default dimensions are 600×400.

{{ placeholder(width=300, height=150) }}

## include

Include the content of another file:

{% raw %}`{% include "file.qmd" %}`{% endraw %}

This is a pre-parse directive --- included content is parsed as blocks, so code chunks and fenced divs work normally. YAML front matter in the included file is stripped automatically.


# Plugin functions

Plugins can register custom Tera functions via the `shortcode` capability in their `plugin.yml`. They are called with the same `{% raw %}{{ name(args) }}{% endraw %}` syntax as built-in functions.

## Example: greeting plugin

`_calepin/plugins/greeting/plugin.yml`:

```yaml
name: greeting
version: 0.1.0
description: "A friendly greeting function"

provides:
  shortcode:
    run: shortcode.py
    names: [greeting]
```

`_calepin/plugins/greeting/shortcode.py`:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, sys

data = json.load(sys.stdin)
name = data["kwargs"].get("name", "World")
fmt = data["format"]

if fmt == "html":
    print(f'<strong>Hello, {name}!</strong>')
elif fmt == "tex":
    print(f'\\textbf{{Hello, {name}!}}')
else:
    print(f'Hello, {name}!')
```

{% raw %}Use it as `{{ greeting(name="Alice") }}`.{% endraw %}

## Input protocol

The executable receives JSON on stdin:

```json
{
  "name": "greeting",
  "args": [],
  "kwargs": {"name": "Alice"},
  "format": "html",
  "meta": {
    "title": "My Document",
    "author": ["Author Name"],
    "date": "2025-01-01"
  }
}
```

## Output protocol

Print the replacement text to stdout. The output is inserted directly at the call site.

- Exit 0: stdout replaces the function call.
- Exit non-zero: a warning is printed, and the function call produces an empty string.

See [Plugins](plugins.qmd) for the full plugin system documentation.


# Resolution order

For each function call, *Calepin* checks:

1. Built-in functions (`pagebreak`, `video`, `brand`, `kbd`, `lipsum`, `placeholder`)
2. Context variables (`meta`, `var`, `env`, `format`)
3. Plugin registry (in front matter order)

Built-in functions take precedence over plugin functions.
